title: Deployment configuration for beelink self-hosted runner

story.i_want_to: create a deployment configuration targeting the beelink self-hosted server, including a deploy script, systemd service unit, nginx reverse proxy config, and runtime configuration
story.so_that: the application can be continuously deployed from GitHub Actions to a self-hosted environment with zero-downtime restarts, TLS termination, and proper process management

rationale: loopctl is initially deployed to a beelink home server before moving to cloud hosting. A robust deploy script with systemd integration ensures the app auto-restarts on crash, logs are captured via journald, and nginx handles TLS termination and reverse proxying. This follows the same deployment pattern as the open_brain project on the same server.

acceptance_criteria.description: A bin/deploy.sh script exists that: pulls latest code from master, compiles a Mix release, runs migrations, and restarts the service
A systemd service unit file (loopctl.service) exists for running the Phoenix release as a daemon with auto-restart
An nginx configuration file exists that reverse-proxies to the Phoenix release on the configured port, with TLS termination for loopctl.local (or configurable domain)
config/runtime.exs reads all production configuration from environment variables: DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY_BASE, PHX_HOST, PORT, CLOAK_KEY
A .env.example file documents all required environment variables with placeholder values
The deploy script runs mix ecto.migrate as part of deployment before restarting the service
The deploy script includes a health check after restart that polls GET /health and fails the deploy if the app does not become healthy within 30 seconds
Mix release configuration is set up in mix.exs with runtime config and appropriate strip_beams settings

test_cases.name: Deploy script is executable and has valid bash syntax
steps: Run bash -n bin/deploy.sh to check syntax
Check file permissions include executable bit
expected_results: Syntax check passes with no errors
File has executable permission

test_cases.name: Systemd service file is valid
steps: Inspect the service file for required directives: ExecStart, Restart, WorkingDirectory, User, EnvironmentFile
expected_results: ExecStart points to the release binary
Restart=on-failure is configured
WorkingDirectory is set to the app directory
EnvironmentFile points to the .env file location

test_cases.name: Nginx config has correct proxy pass
steps: Inspect the nginx config for proxy_pass, upstream, and SSL directives
expected_results: proxy_pass points to http://127.0.0.1:<port>
proxy_set_header Host, X-Real-IP, X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Proto are set
SSL certificate paths are configured (or placeholder comments)

test_cases.name: runtime.exs reads DATABASE_URL from environment
steps: Inspect config/runtime.exs for System.get_env("DATABASE_URL") or System.fetch_env!("DATABASE_URL")
expected_results: DATABASE_URL is read from environment in :prod config_env()
SECRET_KEY_BASE is read from environment
PHX_HOST and PORT are read from environment
CLOAK_KEY is read from environment

test_cases.name: Mix release is configured in mix.exs
steps: Inspect the releases/1 key in the project/0 function
expected_results: A :loopctl release is defined
The release has strip_beams set appropriately

test_cases.name: .env.example documents all required variables
steps: Read .env.example and check for required variable names
expected_results: DATABASE_URL is listed with a placeholder
SECRET_KEY_BASE is listed
CLOAK_KEY is listed
PHX_HOST is listed
PORT is listed

technical_notes: Follow the same deploy pattern as open_brain on beelink (192.168.86.55)
Deploy script should be idempotent and safe to run multiple times
Consider using mix release.init to generate the initial release configuration
The nginx config should include WebSocket upgrade headers for future Phoenix Channel support
The deploy script should set MIX_ENV=prod for compilation and migration
Health check loop: for i in 1..30; curl -sf http://localhost:$PORT/health && break; sleep 1; done
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
